message:
feat: migrate Google Calendar connector to fcp-google-discovery auth
and add provisioning/capability/service-alias integration

Major refactor of the Google Calendar connector to use the shared
fcp-google-discovery authentication infrastructure instead of its own
bespoke auth handling, gaining automatic credential materialization,
scope validation, and service alias resolution.

connectors/google-calendar/Cargo.toml:
- Add fcp-google-discovery dependency for shared auth/provisioning

connectors/google-calendar/src/client.rs (+443/-):
- Refactor client authentication to use GoogleMaterializedAuth from
  fcp-google-discovery instead of the connector-local GoogleCalendarAuth
  enum, enabling automatic token refresh, service account JWT signing,
  and credential ID resolution
- Add google_auth_is_secretless() and google_auth_redacted_label()
  helpers for introspection and self-check reporting

connectors/google-calendar/src/connector.rs (+293/-):
- GoogleCalendarConfig now stores GoogleMaterializedAuth, service
  identity, and required scopes; from_params() becomes async to support
  credential materialization
- Add service_selector support via ServiceAliasRegistry for resolving
  Google service aliases to concrete API configurations
- Add required_scopes tracking with BTreeSet deduplication
- Integrate load_default_google_provisioning_bundle() for self-check
  reporting of provisioning readiness
- Add CapabilityVerifier integration for fine-grained operation
  authorization

Cargo.lock:
- Updated lockfile for new dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
